---
title: "Series 21, Episode 8: Redaction in Action"
author: Christopher Nam
date: '2026-05-31'
slug: series-21-episode-8
categories: [Series 21, Betting]
tags: [Series 21, Betting]
draft: false
output:
  blogdown::html_page:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 1
description: ''
---


```{r, echo = FALSE, include = FALSE}
library(here)
here("static")

preamble_dir <- here("static", "code", "R", "preamble")
preamble_file  <- "post_preamble.R"

source(file.path(preamble_dir, preamble_file))
source(file.path(preamble_dir, "database_preamble.R"))
source(file.path(preamble_dir, "graphics_preamble.R"))
source(file.path(preamble_dir, "google_sheets.R"))

style_dir <- here("static", "code", "R", "style")
source(file.path(style_dir, "ggplot_theme.R"))
source(file.path(style_dir, "reactable_theme.R"))
```

```{r, cache = FALSE}
gs_data_link <- "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1PBYAOnBFOPmpobg2tUg871z64UuQuMNRRcJtw_Y0hZE/edit?usp=sharing"

task_attempt_df <- range_read(gs_data_link, sheet = "Attempts-Tasks") %>%
    filter(!is.na(Series_ID) & Series_ID != "")
        
colnames(task_attempt_df)<-make.names(colnames(task_attempt_df))

contestants_df <- range_read(gs_data_link, sheet = "Contestants")
colnames(contestants_df) <- make.names(colnames(contestants_df))
```


```{r, echo=FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, cache = TRUE}
# Establish the variables and environment for our analysis
mv_var_list <- list(seed_id = 2026-04-09, 
                 sim_max_episode_used = 8,
                 num_sims = 100000,
                 num_eps_series = 10,
                 pr_task_inflate = 3 #Number of pre-recorded tasks in an episode. We need to inflate the sampling by this amount for each episode
)

multiverse_env <- new.env()
invisible(list2env(x = mv_var_list, envir = multiverse_env))

# Performance scripts
perform_dir <- here("static", "code", "R", "historic_performance")
perform_file  <- "historic_performance.R"

sys.source(file.path(perform_dir, perform_file), envir = multiverse_env)

# To calculate the odds through simulation
multi_dir <- here("static", "code", "R", "multiverse")
multi_file  <- "multiverse.R"

sys.source(file.path(multi_dir, multi_file), envir = multiverse_env)
```


# Your Task


> Provide the most <code style="color : black; background-color: black">intriguing and suspenseful</code> commentary on Episode 8 of Series 21 UK Taskmaster. 
> 
> <code style="color : black; background-color: black">The source of all intrigue can be found in the following [Google Sheets File](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1PBYAOnBFOPmpobg2tUg871z64UuQuMNRRcJtw_Y0hZE/edit?usp=sharing)</code>. 

```{r, out.width="40%", fig.cap = "Enter challenger!", fig.show = "hold"}
knitr::include_graphics("https://64.media.tumblr.com/c5079246b9d9d26600f488b38afd19ff/1ade1a42ee7d8bdc-2a/s1280x1920/12fb98eb47dc0c013c2abb7f660e8135b27d2a70.gifv")
```

# Episode Recap

```{r ep-performance-grade, fig.cap = "Episode 8 Performance Report Cards", fig.topcap = FALSE, cache = FALSE}
sys.source(file.path(perform_dir, "ep_performance_table.R"), envir = multiverse_env)
multiverse_env$ep_grade_perf_table
```

:::{.insights}

> Huzzah! Our favourite <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Northern</code> lass won her <code style="color : black; background-color: black">third</code> episode of the series.


As shown in Figure \@ref(fig:ep-performance-grade), our episode performance report card:

- <code style="color : black; background-color: black">I'll fill this in later.</code>
- <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Hopefully the intelligent reader, yes that's you,  all get the joke.</code>
- <code style="color : black; background-color: black">It also creates the desired suspense</code>.
- It's an absolute <code style="color : black; background-color: black">disgrace</code> that <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Amy</code> was only awarded 1 point more than <code style="color : black; background-color: black">the rest of the cast </code> in the <code style="color : black; background-color: black">redaction/censorship task</code>. 
    - <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Armando's</code> was clearly the worst out of all the attempts, something that I'm sure <code style="color : black; background-color: black">he</code> agrees with. 
    - <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Joel's</code> attempt was also far too obvious as to what it was (or was not) censoring, and there was no intrigue or mysterious.
- Our previous favourite (in terms of odds, she'll always be my personal favourite), Amy, won her 3rd episode of the series. She accumulated 19 points, which is considered a very good episode performance.
- However, Joanna and Armando were very closely behind Amy in joint 2nd place. They accumulated 18 points each, which was considered very good and good for Joanna and Armando respectively.
    - Being awarded 5 points for the live studio team task definitely helped this team duo jump up from their joint 3rd position prior to the task.
- Kumail scored 17 points and came in 4th for the episode.
    - This is considered a bad episode for Kumail as it is in the lower half of his episode point distribution.
- Joel ended up in 5th place for the episode, being awarded a 13 points by the Taskmaster.
    - Despite this bottom position, this was a good episode for Joel. He also got to use the other side of the hammer, and grope Alex's bellend, which were likely life highlights for him. 
:::

# Series Scoreboard Tracker

```{r series-tracker, fig.cap = "Series Scoreboard Tracker", out.width = "60%", fig.topcap = FALSE, cache = FALSE}

graph_dir <- here("static", "code", "R", "graphs")
graph_file  <- "series_score_tracker.R"


sys.source(file.path(graph_dir, graph_file), envir = multiverse_env)
multiverse_env$series_score_plot
```

:::{.insights}
> It's a <code style="color : black; background-color: black">bloody outrage</code> that an <code style="color : black; background-color: black">American outrage</code> is surpassing the <code style="color : black; background-color: black">British</code> talent on this show.


As seen in our series tracker (Figure \@ref(fig:series-tracker)):

- <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Again, this whole bit is to a) be humorous (hopefully) b) give me time in writing proper commentary.</code>
- Despite <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Amy's</code> 3rd episode win for the series, she is still only <code style="color : black; background-color: black">3rd</code> place in the series. 
- If <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Joanna </code> wins this series by the skin of <code style="color : black; background-color: black">her</code> teeth (less than 5 points), we can all glance <code style="color : black; background-color: black">with absolute disgust</code> at <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Little Alex Horne and the UK Taskmaster crew</code>.
    - An <code style="color : black; background-color: black">automatic</code> 5 point lead was pratically handed to <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Joanna</code> from <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Episode 1's live task</code> which was <code style="color : black; background-color: black">winner takes all</code>. It quite clearly, provided an <code style="color : black; background-color: black">unfair advantage</code>. 
- There is no change in the series standings compared to those from Episode 7. Amy has closed the gap on Kumail and Joanna. 
    - Joanna is only `r 135-130` points ahead of Amy, a lead that could be easily lost in a single episode.

:::
# Neighbourhood Watch
```{r, cache = FALSE}
library(slider)
s21_ep_rolling_df <- multiverse_env$series_points_df %>% 
    filter(Episode_ID <= multiverse_env$sim_max_episode_used) %>%
    group_by(Series_ID, Contestant) %>%
    arrange(Episode_ID) %>%
    mutate(
        Total_Episodes = multiverse_env$num_eps_series,
        Series_Progress = Episode_ID/Total_Episodes,
        Rolling_Sum_Ep_Score = cumsum(Ep_Points),
        Rolling_Avg_Ep_Score = cummean(Ep_Points),
        Rolling_StdDev_Ep_Score = slide_dbl(Ep_Points, .before = Inf, .f = sd)
    ) %>% 
    #left_join(contestants_df, by = join_by(Contestant == Contestant_Name)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    arrange(Series_ID, Contestant, Episode_ID) %>%
    select(Series_ID, Contestant, Initials, Episode_ID, Ep_Points,
           Series_Points, Series_Ranking,
           Series_Progress, Rolling_Avg_Ep_Score, Rolling_StdDev_Ep_Score,
           Image_URL)

latest_s21_rolling_df <- filter(s21_ep_rolling_df, Episode_ID == multiverse_env$sim_max_episode_used)
```

```{r, cache = FALSE}
all_contestants_df <- range_read(gs_data_link, sheet = "Performance_History_Contestants",
                                 range = "B:AF")
colnames(all_contestants_df) <- make.names(colnames(all_contestants_df))


all_contestants_df$Position_in_Series <- as.factor(all_contestants_df$Position_in_Series)

key_cols <- c("Series", "Contestant", "Initals", "Label", "Position_in_Series", "Total_Episodes")


# All historical episode scores for regular series of Taskmaster UK
full_hist_ep_score_df <- all_contestants_df %>% select(key_cols, starts_with("Ep")) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("Ep"),
                 names_to = "Variable",
                 values_to = "Ep_Score"
    ) %>% 
    mutate(
        Episode_ID = parse_number(Variable),
        Series_Progress = Episode_ID/Total_Episodes
        ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Ep_Score)) %>%
    group_by(across(key_cols)) %>%
    arrange(Episode_ID) %>%
    mutate(
        Rolling_Avg_Ep_Score = cummean(Ep_Score),
        Rolling_StdDev_Ep_Score = slide_dbl(Ep_Score, .before = Inf, .f = sd)
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>% 
    arrange(Series, Contestant, Episode_ID) %>%
    select(-Variable)

is_outlier <- function(x) {
  return(x < quantile(x, 0.25) - 1.5 * IQR(x) | x > quantile(x, 0.75) + 1.5 * IQR(x))
}


closest_ep_score_df <- mutate(full_hist_ep_score_df,
       Ref_Series_Progress = unique(latest_s21_rolling_df$Series_Progress),
       Delta_Series_Progress = abs(Series_Progress - Ref_Series_Progress)
) %>% group_by(Series, Contestant, Initals, Label) %>%
    slice_min(order_by = Delta_Series_Progress) %>%
    slice_max(order_by = Episode_ID) %>%
    group_by(Position_in_Series) %>%
    mutate(
        boxplot_outlier_label = ifelse(is_outlier(Rolling_Avg_Ep_Score), Label, NA)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
```

```{r avg-sd-scatter, fig.cap = "Scatter! A graphical visualisation of Contestants Episode Performance and their Volatility.", cache = FALSE}
series_progess_pc <- percent(unique(closest_ep_score_df$Ref_Series_Progress))

title_label <- "Scatterplot of Contestants, Their Performance and Volatility"
subtitle_label <- glue("Performance measured in Average Episode Score, Volatility measured in Standard Deviation at {pc} of Series Progress", pc = series_progess_pc)

cloud_plot <- ggplot(closest_ep_score_df, aes(x = Rolling_Avg_Ep_Score , y= Rolling_StdDev_Ep_Score )) +
    geom_image(data = latest_s21_rolling_df, aes(x= Rolling_Avg_Ep_Score , y = Rolling_StdDev_Ep_Score, image =  Image_URL ), size = 0.05, alpha = 0.5, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    geom_point(aes(colour = Position_in_Series, shape = Position_in_Series), alpha = 0.5, size = 2) +
#    geom_text_repel(aes(label = Label), max.overlaps = 25, alpha = 0.75, size = 5) +
    
    quack_theme() +
    labs(title = title_label, 
         subtitle = subtitle_label, 
         x = "Average Episode Score",
         y = "Standard Deviation of Episode Scores",
         shape = "Position in Series",
         colour = "Position in Series"
         ) +
    scale_x_continuous(n.breaks = 10, minor_breaks = waiver()) +
    scale_y_continuous(n.breaks = 10, minor_breaks = waiver())
```

```{r sim-neighbourhood, fig.cap = "Won't you be my Neighbour? Who are the neighbours of the cast of Series 21? Neighbours are based on a similarity measure based on average episode score and volatility.", cache = FALSE}
max_sim_match <- 5

euclid_sim_df <-cross_join(latest_s21_rolling_df, closest_ep_score_df) %>%
    mutate(Euclid_Distance = sqrt((Rolling_Avg_Ep_Score.x - Rolling_Avg_Ep_Score.y)^2 +  (Rolling_StdDev_Ep_Score.x - Rolling_StdDev_Ep_Score.y)^2)
    ) %>% group_by(Series_ID, Contestant.x, Initials) %>%
    mutate(Distance_Rank = rank(Euclid_Distance)) %>% 
    arrange(Distance_Rank) %>%
    filter(Distance_Rank <= max_sim_match)


title_label <- glue("The Neighbourhood Watch for the Cast of Series 21 at {pc} of Series Progress", pc = series_progess_pc)
subtitle_label <- "Neighbourhood is defined by similarity to previous contestants of UK Taskmaster"
caption_label <- "Simiarlity defined on mean episode score and standard deviation"


hood_plot <- ggplot(euclid_sim_df, aes(x = Rolling_Avg_Ep_Score.y, y = Rolling_StdDev_Ep_Score.y)) +
    geom_image(aes(x = Rolling_Avg_Ep_Score.x, y= Rolling_StdDev_Ep_Score.x, image = Image_URL), size = 0.1) +
    geom_point(aes(colour = Position_in_Series, shape = Position_in_Series), size = 2, alpha = 0.75) +
    facet_wrap(vars(Contestant.x), scales = "free") +
    geom_text_repel(aes(label = Label), na.rm = TRUE, min.segment.length = 0, size = 5, alpha = 0.75) +
    labs(x = "Average Episode Score",
         y= "Stardard Deviation Episode Score",
         title = title_label,
         subtitle = subtitle_label,
         caption = caption_label,
         colour = "Position in Series",
         shape = "Position in Series"
    ) +
    quack_theme() 
```

```{r cloud-hood, out.width="49%", fig.show = "hold", fig.cap = "Left: Scatter! A graphical visualisation of Contestants Episode Performance and their Volatility. <br> Right: The similar neighbours of the cast of Series 21."}
cloud_plot
hood_plot
```

:::{.insights}
> <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Surprise! Thought you knew the structure of these posts right?</code>


From Figure \@ref(fig:cloud-hood):

- <code style="color : black; background-color: black">To keep you on your toes, I've reduced the real estate for these
plots which may be a surprise for regular readers.</code>.
- <code style="color : black; background-color: black">I've yet to figure out how to incorporate these plots into the commentary, and whether they provide any real value.</code>.
- Amy and Kumail are practically neighbours now! 
    - They share 4 out of 5 common neighbours: Series 13's Chris Ramsey (2nd), Series 11's Sarah Kendall (1st), Series 5's Aisling Bea (4th) and Series 8's Lou Sanders (1st).
    - Amy is graced by the presence of Series 1's Romesh Ranganathan who placed 2nd his series.
    - Kumail has the bundle of joy that is  Series 18's Jack Dee, who placed 2nd in his series as well.
- Joanna now has a series champion in her neighbourhood! The original series champion, Series 1's Josh Widdicombe, moved into Joanna's neighbourhood, complete with his karate trophy (he famously didn't get a Greg head for as the first series champion), and Greg's headless body (from winning Champion of Champions I).
- Joel still lives in a neighbourhood that is predominantly filled with series losers. 3 out of 5 neighbours placed 5th in their series, and the remaining 2 neighbours placed 4th.
- Armando still has a mixed bag in his neighbourhood. Richard Herring still provdes
:::
# Gamble's Gamble

## End-of-Series Ranking Distributions
```{r series-rankings, fig.cap = "Series Ranking Distribution: Using Data up to Episode 7 (left), and up to Episode 8 (right).", out.width = "49%", fig.show='hold', cache = FALSE}


knitr::include_graphics("https://bluevolvo87.github.io/themedianduck/2026/05/series-21-episode-7/index_files/figure-html/series-rankings-2.png")

sys.source(file.path(graph_dir, "series_ranking_distribution.R"), envir = multiverse_env)

multiverse_env$series_ranking_dist_plot
```

:::{.insights}
> <code style="color : black; background-color: black">None of the within episode distributions figures from now on as they don't provide much value towards the end of the series. 
>
> I could compare them with the empirical episode ranking we have observed as a useful, sanity check...</code>

The series ranking distributions (Figure \@ref(fig:series-rankings)) show that:

- Things have <code style="color : black; background-color: black">perked</code> up a bit for <code style="color : black; background-color: black">our Amy</code>.
- Hopefully <code style="color : black; background-color: black">our Amy</code> can <code style="color : black; background-color: black">kick</code> Kumail's <code style="color : black; background-color: black">fat Hollywood arse</code> back into <code style="color : black; background-color: black">3rd</code> place.
- Whilst <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Amy's</code> at it, <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Joanna</code> also deserves <code style="color : black; background-color: black">taking down a peg or two</code>. 


:::

### The Ever Changing Series Distributions

```{r dist-animate, fig.cap = "Animation and evolution of the series ranking distributions as the series progresses.", out.width = "60%", echo = FALSE}

rep_factor <- 2
morph_factor <- 4
animation_fps <- 4


dist_vec <- c("https://bluevolvo87.github.io/themedianduck/2026/04/series-21-episode-1/index_files/figure-html/series-rankings-1.png",
              "https://bluevolvo87.github.io/themedianduck/2026/04/series-21-episode-2/index_files/figure-html/series-rankings-2.png",
              "https://bluevolvo87.github.io/themedianduck/2026/04/series-21-episode-3/index_files/figure-html/series-rankings-2.png",
              "https://bluevolvo87.github.io/themedianduck/2026/05/series-21-episode-4/index_files/figure-html/series-rankings-2.png",
              "https://bluevolvo87.github.io/themedianduck/2026/05/series-21-episode-5/index_files/figure-html/series-rankings-2.png",
              "https://bluevolvo87.github.io/themedianduck/2026/05/series-21-episode-6/index_files/figure-html/series-rankings-2.png",
              "https://bluevolvo87.github.io/themedianduck/2026/05/series-21-episode-7/index_files/figure-html/series-rankings-2.png"
)

dist_vec <- rep(dist_vec, each = rep_factor)


current_dist <- image_read(
    rep(file.path(here("content", "posts", "2026-05-31-series-21-episode-8", "index_files", "figure-html"),  "series-rankings-2.png"),
        each = rep_factor)
)

current_dist_end <- image_annotate(current_dist[1], glue("END \n (after Ep {ep})", ep = multiverse_env$sim_max_episode_used), size = 200, color = "darkred", font = "Helvetica", weight = 700, gravity = "Center")

dist_full_seq <- image_read(dist_vec)

dist_start <- image_annotate(dist_full_seq[1], glue("START \n (after Ep 1)"), size = 200, color = "darkgreen", font = "Helvetica", weight = 700, gravity = "Center")

frames <- image_join(dist_start, 
                     dist_full_seq,
                     current_dist,
                     current_dist_end)

animation <- image_animate(image_morph(frames, frames = morph_factor), fps = animation_fps, optimize = TRUE)

animation
```
> <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Moving images! All because someone put the lime in the co-conker-nut... </code>


## Joint Cast Ranking Distributions
```{r joint-cast-ranking, fig.cap = "Joint Distribution of Cast Series Ranking: Using Data up to Episode 7 (left), and up to Episode 8 (right).", out.width = "49%", fig.show='hold', cache = FALSE}

knitr::include_graphics("https://bluevolvo87.github.io/themedianduck/2026/05/series-21-episode-7/index_files/figure-html/joint-cast-ranking-2.png")

assign("prev_ep_joint_pred", c(0, 0, 0, 0, 0), envir = multiverse_env) # To pass on a previous prediction
sys.source(file.path(graph_dir, "joint_cast_ranking_distribution.R"), envir = multiverse_env)

multiverse_env$joint_cast_ranking_dist_plot
```

:::{.insights}
> <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Not much change here.</code>

In terms of the full cast ranking outcome (Figure \@ref(fig:joint-cast-ranking)):

- There's not much change in the most <code style="color : black; background-color: black">probable</code> outcome. 
    - This is <code style="color : black; background-color: black">not the biggest surprise</code> given that the <code style="color : black; background-color: black">series rankings</code> have remained the same as <code style="color : black; background-color: black">last episode</code>.
- However, I'm still not entirely <code style="color : black; background-color: black">happy</code> that the <code style="color : black; background-color: black">first combination</code> in which <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Amy</code> wins is <code style="color : black; background-color: black">the 5th most probable</code>.
- <code style="color : black; background-color: black">I don't know why exactly I am getting so stressed and anxious about Amy not being crowned champion. It's not like I placed a huge monetary bet of Amy being crowned the Series 21 champion.</code>
    - <code style="color : black; background-color: black">I guess I might be getting stressed as I feel my statistical reputation is at stake, even though my reputation is non-existent these days</code>.

:::

```{r, out.width="40%", fig.cap = "All this <code style='color : black; background-color: black'>censorship</code> and <code style='color : black; background-color: black'>redaction</code> is making me so <code style='color : black; background-color: black'>hot</code>!", fig.show = "hold"}

knitr::include_graphics("https://64.media.tumblr.com/8e3d2e97c39171ed9b2732c3b3dd74d8/2a1013298fee5518-cd/s1280x1920/dedb185d63d217eea245da652f55260f98f58cc9.gifv")

```

# What Have We Learnt Today?




:::{.infobox .today}
We've learnt that:

- <code style="color : black; background-color: black">The author of The Median Duck has a sense of humour. Or at least he thinks he has</code>.
- Little Alex Horne and the crew of UK Taskmaster should be <code style="color : black; background-color: black">ashamed</code> of themselves if <code style="color : black; background-color: black">Joanna</code> wins the series with <code style="color : black; background-color: black">anything less than a 5 point lead</code>.
- <code style="color : black; background-color: black">The author of The Median Duck</code> is <code style="color : black; background-color: black">trying out a new</code> publishing strategy <code style="color : black; background-color: black">in which the main graphs and tables</code> are posted first, and then the <code style="color : black; background-color: black">written commentary</code> is added later.
    - I have a feeling that <code style="color : black; background-color: black"> no one really reads the written commentary</code>, and everyone <code style="color : black; background-color: black">mainly just looks at the graphs</code>.
    - This may even <code style="color : black; background-color: black">reduce my stress and anxiety.</code>
:::

```{r, out.width="40%", fig.cap = "All these redactions require no explanation...", fig.show = "hold"}

knitr::include_graphics("https://64.media.tumblr.com/6d1847fd088c0502406c7d0ae9f9e76a/d0ae6d9b1139c008-12/s1280x1920/d3fe33fd8d5cdb6cd936b420d811b05248c4c21d.png")

```
